content.problemStatement:
Given the time control of a chess match as a+ba + ba+b, determine which format of chess out of the given 444 it belongs to.

1)1)1) Bullet if a+b<3a + b \lt 3a+b<3

2)2)2) Blitz if 3≤a+b≤103 \leq a + b \leq 103≤a+b≤10

3)3)3) Rapid if 11≤a+b≤6011 \leq a + b \leq 6011≤a+b≤60

4)4)4) Classical if 60<a+b60 \lt a + b60<a+b


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
Each testcase contains a single line of input, two integers a,ba, ba,b.


Output Format
For each testcase, output in a single line, answer 111 for bullet, 222 for blitz, 333 for rapid, and 444 for classical format.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers a and b.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single integer on its own line: 1 for Bullet, 2 for Blitz, 3 for Rapid, and 4 for Classical.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
For each pair (a,b) we compute a+b:
- 1+0=1 < 3 → Bullet → 1
- 4+1=5 falls in [3,10] → Blitz → 2
- 100+0=100 > 60 → Classical → 4
- 20+5=25 falls in [11,60] → Rapid → 3

hidden.label: edge case at lower bound of Blitz
edge case at upper bound of Blitz
edge case at upper bound of Rapid

solution.approach:
For each test case compute total = a + b. Then use a chain of if‑else statements:
- if total < 3 → print 1
- else if total <= 10 → print 2
- else if total <= 60 → print 3
- else → print 4
The algorithm runs in O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time check per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
